1	\chapter{Effective descent: restriction of the glued sheaf to a chart}
2	\label{chap:Picard_GlueDescent}
3	
4	\providecommand{\ShMod}{\mathrm{SheafOfModules}}
5	
6	The gluing primitive \cref{def:scheme_modules_glue} produces, out of a glue datum
7	\(D\) of schemes (index set \(J\), charts \(U_i\), overlaps \(V_{ij}\), overlap
8	immersions \(f_{ij} : V_{ij} \hookrightarrow U_i\), transition isomorphisms
9	\(t_{ij} : V_{ij} \xrightarrow{\sim} V_{ji}\), glued scheme \(X\) with chart
10	immersions \(\iota_i : U_i \hookrightarrow X\)), a sheaf of \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules
11	\(\mathcal{M}\) as the descent equalizer
12	\[
13	  \mathcal{M} \;=\; \operatorname{eq}(a, b)
14	    \;\hookrightarrow\; \prod_{i} (\iota_i)_{*}\,\mathcal{M}_i
15	\]
16	of two parallel maps into the overlap product. That construction makes
17	\(\mathcal{M}\) but does \emph{not} yet consume the cocycle conditions (C1)/(C2) on
18	the transition family \((g_{ij})\). This chapter discharges the remaining content of
19	descent: it shows that restricting the glued sheaf back to a chart recovers the
20	input sheaf there. Concretely, the canonical \emph{restriction morphism}
21	\(\iota_i^{*}\mathcal{M} \to \mathcal{M}_i\) -- the adjoint transpose along the chart
22	immersion \(\iota_i\) of the \(i\)-th equalizer projection -- is an isomorphism, and
23	it is here that (C1)/(C2) are used. The engine is that pullback along an open
24	immersion is, up to natural isomorphism, the site-level restriction functor
25	(\cref{lem:modules_restrictFunctorIsoPullback_mathlib}); being a right adjoint it
26	preserves the descent equalizer and the pushforward product, so the chart
27	restriction of \(\mathcal{M}\) is computed factor by factor. The non-formal input is
28	the \emph{overlap base change} \(\beta_{ij}\), which identifies the restricted
29	pushforward factor \(\iota_i^{*}(\iota_j)_{*}\mathcal{M}_j\) with a pushforward of a
30	restriction of \(\mathcal{M}_j\) to the overlap, using the cartesianness of the
31	chart--overlap square.
32	
33	\section{The descent equalizer and its overlap base change}
34	\label{sec:gd_equalizer}
35	
36	\begin{definition}
37	
38	\leanok
39	[The pushforward product carrying the descent equalizer]
40	  \label{def:gr_glue_equalizer}
41	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.glueProd}
42	  \uses{def:scheme_modules_glue}
43	  Let \(D\) be a glue datum and \((\mathcal{M}_i)_{i \in J}\) a family of sheaves of
44	  modules with \(\mathcal{M}_i\) on the chart \(U_i\). Write
45	  \[
46	    P \;=\; \prod_{i} (\iota_i)_{*}\,\mathcal{M}_i
47	    \qquad\text{and}\qquad
48	    Q \;=\; \prod_{(i,j)} (f_{ij} \circ \iota_i)_{*}\,\bigl(f_{ij}^{*}\mathcal{M}_i\bigr)
49	  \]
50	  for the \emph{pushforward product} and the \emph{overlap product} of sheaves of
51	  \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules. The two descent legs of \cref{def:scheme_modules_glue},
52	  \[
53	    P \;\underset{b}{\overset{a}{\rightrightarrows}}\; Q,
54	  \]
55	  are: on the \((i,j)\)-component, leg \(a\) restricts the \(i\)-th chart factor to
56	  the overlap \(V_{ij}\) using the unit of the pullback--pushforward adjunction along
57	  \(f_{ij}\) and the pushforward-composition comparison
58	  \((\iota_i)_{*} \cong (f_{ij} \circ \iota_i)_{*} \circ f_{ij}^{*}\); leg \(b\)
59	  restricts the \(j\)-th chart factor, transports it across the inverse transition
60	  isomorphism \(g_{ij}^{-1}\), and reindexes the immersion via the glue condition
61	  \((t_{ij} \circ f_{ji}) \circ \iota_j = f_{ij} \circ \iota_i\). The glued sheaf
62	  \(\mathcal{M} = \operatorname{eq}(a,b)\) is the equalizer of this parallel pair;
63	  \(P\) is the object it embeds into and \(Q\) the object detecting the overlap
64	  agreement. The product \(P\) is the carrier on which the \(i\)-th projection (and,
65	  through it, the restriction morphism) is defined.
66	\end{definition}
67	
68	\begin{lemma}
69	
70	\leanok
71	[Overlap base change of the chart square (\(\beta_{ij}\))]
72	  \label{lem:glueOverlapBaseChangeIso}
73	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.glueOverlapBaseChangeIso}
74	  \uses{lem:modules_restrictFunctorIsoPullback_mathlib}
75	  For chart indices \(i, j \in J\) there is a natural isomorphism of functors
76	  \(\ShMod(\mathcal{O}_{U_j}) \to \ShMod(\mathcal{O}_{U_i})\),
77	  \[
78	    \beta_{ij} \;:\;
79	      \iota_i^{*}\,(\iota_j)_{*}
80	        \;\xrightarrow{\ \sim\ }\;
81	      (f_{ij})_{*}\,(t_{ij} \circ f_{ji})^{*},
82	  \]
83	  identifying, for any sheaf \(\mathcal{N}\) on the chart \(U_j\), the restriction to
84	  the chart \(U_i\) of the pushforward \((\iota_j)_{*}\mathcal{N}\) with the
85	  pushforward along \(f_{ij}\) of the restriction of \(\mathcal{N}\) to the overlap
86	  \(V_{ij}\) along \(t_{ij} \circ f_{ji}\). Here the chart restriction \(\iota_i^{*}\)
87	  and the overlap restriction \((t_{ij} \circ f_{ji})^{*}\) are realised as the
88	  site-level restriction functors, isomorphic to the geometric pullbacks by
89	  \cref{lem:modules_restrictFunctorIsoPullback_mathlib}.
90	\end{lemma}
91	\begin{proof}
92	  \uses{lem:modules_restrictFunctorIsoPullback_mathlib, lem:gr_overlap_appIso_compat,
93	    lem:gr_overlapBaseChange_inv_app, lem:gr_overlapBaseChange_hom_app}
94	    \leanok
95	  The isomorphism is the cartesianness of the chart--overlap square, made site-level.
96	  The square
97	  \[
98	    \begin{array}{ccc}
99	      V_{ij} & \xrightarrow{\ t_{ij} \circ f_{ji}\ } & U_j \\
100	      \ \downarrow{\scriptstyle f_{ij}} & & \downarrow{\scriptstyle \iota_j} \\
101	      U_i & \xrightarrow{\ \ \iota_i\ \ } & X
102	    \end{array}
103	  \]
104	  is cartesian, and its cartesianness is visible already on underlying opens: for an
105	  open \(V \subseteq U_i\),
106	  \[
107	    \iota_j^{-1}\bigl(\iota_i(V)\bigr)
108	      \;=\; (t_{ij} \circ f_{ji})\bigl(f_{ij}^{-1}(V)\bigr),
109	  \]
110	  a point of \(U_j\) lands in the image of \(V\) under \(\iota_i\) precisely when it
111	  comes, via the glue relation, from a point of the overlap lying over \(V\). Hence
112	  the two composite opens functors
113	  \[
114	    (\iota_i)_{!} \circ \iota_j^{-1}
115	      \;=\;
116	    f_{ij}^{-1} \circ (t_{ij} \circ f_{ji})_{!}
117	  \]
118	  agree on objects, and -- morphisms of the opens poset being proof-irrelevant -- as
119	  functors. Both \(\iota_i^{*}(\iota_j)_{*}\) and \((f_{ij})_{*}(t_{ij}\circ
120	  f_{ji})^{*}\) are site-level pushforwards along these two (equal) opens functors, so
121	  the comparison is assembled from the pushforward-composition isomorphisms together
122	  with the natural isomorphism transporting one opens functor to the other:
123	  \[
124	    \beta_{ij}
125	      \;=\;
126	    (\text{pushforwardComp})
127	      \;\circ\;
128	    (\text{pushforward of the opens-functor equality})
129	      \;\circ\;
130	    (\text{pushforwardCongr})
131	      \;\circ\;
132	    (\text{pushforwardComp})^{-1}.
133	  \]
134	  The one substantive coherence to check is that the comparison glued from the
135	  object-level opens equality is natural in the open \(V\): on the section level the
136	  two restriction maps induced by the equal composite functors coincide because the
137	  connecting morphisms are the unique arrows of the opens poset between the identified
138	  opens. This is the pointwise compatibility that completes the \(\mathrm{pushforwardCongr}\)
139	  factor.
140	\end{proof}
141	
142	\section{The restriction morphism and effective descent}
143	\label{sec:gd_descent}
144	
145	\begin{lemma}
146	
147	\leanok
148	[The chart restriction morphism of the glued sheaf]
149	  \label{lem:glueRestrictionHom}
150	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.glueRestrictionHom}
151	  \uses{def:gr_glue_equalizer, def:scheme_modules_glue}
152	  Let \(\mathcal{M}\) be the glued sheaf of \cref{def:scheme_modules_glue}. The
153	  \(i\)-th \emph{projection} of \(\mathcal{M}\),
154	  \[
155	    \pi_i \;:\; \mathcal{M} \longrightarrow (\iota_i)_{*}\,\mathcal{M}_i,
156	  \]
157	  is the equalizer inclusion \(\mathcal{M} \hookrightarrow P\)
158	  (\cref{def:gr_glue_equalizer}) followed by the \(i\)-th product projection. Its
159	  adjoint transpose along the chart immersion \(\iota_i\), under the
160	  pullback--pushforward adjunction \(\iota_i^{*} \dashv (\iota_i)_{*}\), is the
161	  \emph{restriction morphism}
162	  \[
163	    \mathrm{r}_i \;:\; \iota_i^{*}\,\mathcal{M} \longrightarrow \mathcal{M}_i .
164	  \]
165	  Equivalently, \(\mathrm{r}_i\) is the unique morphism whose pushforward, precomposed
166	  with the unit, recovers \(\pi_i\).
167	\end{lemma}
168	\begin{proof}
169	  \uses{def:gr_glue_equalizer, def:scheme_modules_glue}
170	  \leanok
171	  The projection \(\pi_i\) is well-defined by \cref{def:gr_glue_equalizer}: the glued
172	  sheaf is the equalizer \(\operatorname{eq}(a,b) \hookrightarrow P\), and composing
173	  the inclusion with the \(i\)-th projection of the product \(P = \prod_i
174	  (\iota_i)_{*}\mathcal{M}_i\) gives a map to \((\iota_i)_{*}\mathcal{M}_i\). The
175	  restriction morphism is then its image under the natural bijection
176	  \[
177	    \operatorname{Hom}_{\mathcal{O}_X}\bigl(\mathcal{M}, (\iota_i)_{*}\mathcal{M}_i\bigr)
178	      \;\cong\;
179	    \operatorname{Hom}_{\mathcal{O}_{U_i}}\bigl(\iota_i^{*}\mathcal{M}, \mathcal{M}_i\bigr)
180	  \]
181	  supplied by the adjunction \(\iota_i^{*} \dashv (\iota_i)_{*}\). The stated
182	  equivalent description is the unit--counit form of this transpose.
183	\end{proof}
184	
185	\begin{theorem}
186	
187	\leanok
188	[Effective descent: the chart restriction morphism is an isomorphism]
189	  \label{thm:isIso_glueRestrictionHom}
190	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.isIso_glueRestrictionHom}
191	  \uses{lem:glueRestrictionHom, lem:glueOverlapBaseChangeIso,
192	    lem:isLimitPullbackCone_mathlib, lem:eq_of_locally_eq_mathlib,
193	    def:gr_glueRestrictionInv, lem:gr_glueRestrict_hom_ext,
194	    lem:gr_glueChartComponent_self_counit,
195	    lem:gr_glueRestrictionHom_glueChartComponent,
196	    lem:gr_glueRestrictionInv_proj}
197	  Assume the transition family \((g_{ij})\) satisfies the cocycle conditions (C1) and
198	  (C2) of \cref{def:scheme_modules_glue}. Then for every chart index \(i\) the
199	  restriction morphism \(\mathrm{r}_i : \iota_i^{*}\mathcal{M} \to \mathcal{M}_i\)
200	  (\cref{lem:glueRestrictionHom}) is an isomorphism of sheaves of
201	  \(\mathcal{O}_{U_i}\)-modules.
202	\end{theorem}
203	\begin{proof}
204	  \uses{lem:glueRestrictionHom, lem:glueOverlapBaseChangeIso,
205	    lem:isLimitPullbackCone_mathlib, lem:eq_of_locally_eq_mathlib,
206	    def:gr_glueRestrictionInv, lem:gr_glueRestrict_hom_ext,
207	    lem:gr_glueChartComponent_self_counit,
208	    lem:gr_glueRestrictionHom_glueChartComponent,
209	    lem:gr_glueRestrictionInv_proj}
210	    \leanok
211	  The chart pullback \(\iota_i^{*}\) is naturally isomorphic to the site-level
212	  restriction functor (\cref{lem:modules_restrictFunctorIsoPullback_mathlib}), which
213	  is a right adjoint; consequently \(\iota_i^{*}\) \emph{preserves limits}. Applying
214	  it to the descent equalizer \(\mathcal{M} = \operatorname{eq}(a,b)\) exhibits
215	  \(\iota_i^{*}\mathcal{M}\) as the equalizer of the restricted legs
216	  \(\iota_i^{*}a, \iota_i^{*}b\), and applying it to the product
217	  \(P = \prod_j (\iota_j)_{*}\mathcal{M}_j\) exhibits \(\iota_i^{*}P\) as the product
218	  \(\prod_j \iota_i^{*}(\iota_j)_{*}\mathcal{M}_j\). The overlap base change
219	  \(\beta_{ij}\) of \cref{lem:glueOverlapBaseChangeIso} then identifies the \(j\)-th
220	  factor \(\iota_i^{*}(\iota_j)_{*}\mathcal{M}_j\) with
221	  \((f_{ij})_{*}\bigl((t_{ij} \circ f_{ji})^{*}\mathcal{M}_j\bigr)\). Under these
222	  identifications the restricted descent data becomes the data attached to the chart
223	  \(U_i\) by the cover \(\{f_{ij}\}\) of \(U_i\), and the \(i\)-th factor (where
224	  \(j = i\)) is, by (C1), \(\mathcal{M}_i\) itself.
225	
226	  We produce an inverse \(\mathrm{s}_i : \mathcal{M}_i \to \iota_i^{*}\mathcal{M}\).
227	  Because \(\iota_i^{*}\mathcal{M}\) is the equalizer of the restricted legs, it
228	  suffices to give a morphism \(\mathcal{M}_i \to \iota_i^{*}P\) -- equivalently a
229	  compatible family of morphisms into the factors -- that equalizes
230	  \(\iota_i^{*}a\) and \(\iota_i^{*}b\). The \(j\)-th component is
231	  \[
232	    \mathcal{M}_i
233	      \xrightarrow{\ \eta_{f_{ij}}\ }
234	    (f_{ij})_{*}\,f_{ij}^{*}\mathcal{M}_i
235	      \xrightarrow{\ (f_{ij})_{*} g_{ij}\ }
236	    (f_{ij})_{*}\,(t_{ij} \circ f_{ji})^{*}\mathcal{M}_j
237	      \xrightarrow{\ \beta_{ij}^{-1}\ }
238	    \iota_i^{*}(\iota_j)_{*}\mathcal{M}_j ,
239	  \]
240	  the unit of the pullback--pushforward adjunction along \(f_{ij}\), followed by the
241	  pushforward of the transition isomorphism \(g_{ij}\), followed by the inverse of the
242	  overlap base change. That this family equalizes the two restricted legs is exactly
243	  the triple-overlap multiplicativity (C2), transported through \(\beta\); and the
244	  composite \(\mathrm{r}_i \circ \mathrm{s}_i = \mathrm{id}_{\mathcal{M}_i}\) reduces,
245	  via the \(j = i\) component, to the self-identity (C1) together with the counit
246	  triangle identity of the adjunction. The reverse composite
247	  \(\mathrm{s}_i \circ \mathrm{r}_i = \mathrm{id}\) holds because both sides are
248	  equalizer maps with the same components into \(P\); two such agree once their
249	  factors agree, which is the separatedness of the sheaf equalizer over the chart
250	  cover. The limit computation underlying these equalities is the sheaf
251	  Mayer--Vietoris pullback square (\cref{lem:isLimitPullbackCone_mathlib}), and the
252	  final identification of sections is the uniqueness-over-a-cover principle
253	  (\cref{lem:eq_of_locally_eq_mathlib}). Hence \(\mathrm{r}_i\) is an isomorphism.
254	\end{proof}
255	
256	\section{Construction of the inverse: the descent obligations}
257	\label{sec:gd_inverse}
258	
259	This section records, as sublemmas of \cref{thm:isIso_glueRestrictionHom}, the
260	internal machinery of the proof: the structure-sheaf compatibilities feeding the
261	overlap base change \(\beta_{ij}\), the bridges between the geometric and the
262	site-level pullback--pushforward adjunctions, the explicit construction of the
263	candidate inverse \(\mathrm{s}_i\) as an equalizer lift, and the three obligations
264	that consume the cocycle conditions (C1)/(C2). Every block carries a one-to-one Lean
265	declaration.
266	
267	\subsection{Section-level compatibilities of the overlap square}
268	
269	\begin{lemma}\leanok
270	[Transport of \(\mathrm{appLE}\) along equal morphisms]
271	  \label{lem:gr_appLE_congr_mor}
272	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.appLE_congr_mor}
273	  Let \(f, g : A \to B\) be morphisms of schemes with \(f = g\), let \(U\) be an open of
274	  \(B\) and \(W\) an open of \(A\) with \(W \subseteq f^{-1}(U)\) (equivalently
275	  \(W \subseteq g^{-1}(U)\)). Then the two induced section maps
276	  \(\Gamma(B, U) \to \Gamma(A, W)\) along \(f\) and along \(g\) coincide:
277	  \(f^{\mathrm{LE}}_{U,W} = g^{\mathrm{LE}}_{U,W}\).
278	\end{lemma}
279	\begin{proof}
280	
281	\leanok
282	  Substituting the equality \(f = g\) reduces both sides to the same map; the two
283	  open-inclusion witnesses \(W \subseteq f^{-1}(U)\) and \(W \subseteq g^{-1}(U)\) are
284	  proof-irrelevant.
285	\end{proof}
286	
287	\begin{lemma}\leanok
288	[Structure-sheaf compatibility of the chart--overlap square]
289	  \label{lem:gr_overlap_appIso_compat}
290	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.glueData_overlap_appIso_compat}
291	  \uses{lem:gr_appLE_congr_mor, def:scheme_modules_glue}
292	  For chart indices \(i, j\) and an open \(V \subseteq U_i\), the two composite
293	  section maps
294	  \[
295	    \Gamma(U_i, V)\;\longrightarrow\;
296	      \Gamma\bigl(U_j,\ (t_{ij}\circ f_{ji})\bigl(f_{ij}^{-1}(V)\bigr)\bigr)
297	  \]
298	  of the chart--overlap square agree. The first goes ``through the glued scheme'':
299	  the inverse of the comparison isomorphism of \(\iota_i\) on \(V\), the section map of
300	  \(\iota_j\) over \(\iota_i(V)\), and the restriction along the opens identity
301	  \(\iota_j^{-1}(\iota_i(V)) = (t_{ij}\circ f_{ji})(f_{ij}^{-1}(V))\). The second goes
302	  ``through the overlap'': the section map of \(f_{ij}\) over \(V\) followed by the
303	  inverse comparison isomorphism of \(t_{ij}\circ f_{ji}\).
304	\end{lemma}
305	\begin{proof}
306	  \uses{lem:gr_appLE_congr_mor, def:scheme_modules_glue}
307	  \leanok
308	  Both composites are \(\mathrm{appLE}\)s of the two composite morphisms of the square,
309	  which are equal by the glue condition \((t_{ij}\circ f_{ji})\circ \iota_j =
310	  f_{ij}\circ \iota_i\) of \cref{def:scheme_modules_glue}. Moving the two comparison
311	  isomorphisms to \(\mathrm{appLE}\) form (using \(\mathrm{appIso}\) as an
312	  \(\mathrm{appLE}\), the composition law \(g^{\mathrm{LE}}\circ f^{\mathrm{LE}} =
313	  (f\circ g)^{\mathrm{LE}}\), and the absorption of presheaf restrictions into the
314	  open-inclusion witness) reduces the claim to the equality of the two
315	  \(\mathrm{appLE}\)s of the equal composites, which is \cref{lem:gr_appLE_congr_mor}.
316	\end{proof}
317	
318	\begin{lemma}\leanok
319	[Sections of the overlap base change, inverse side]
320	  \label{lem:gr_overlapBaseChange_inv_app}
321	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.glueOverlapBaseChangeIso_inv_app_app}
322	  \uses{lem:glueOverlapBaseChangeIso}
323	  For a sheaf \(\mathcal{N}\) on \(U_j\) and an open \(V \subseteq U_i\), the inverse
324	  of the overlap base change \(\beta_{ij}^{-1}\), evaluated on sections over \(V\), is
325	  the restriction map of \(\mathcal{N}\) along the opens identity
326	  \(\iota_j^{-1}(\iota_i(V)) = (t_{ij}\circ f_{ji})(f_{ij}^{-1}(V))\) of
327	  \cref{lem:glueOverlapBaseChangeIso}:
328	  \[
329	    (\beta_{ij}^{-1})_{\mathcal{N}}\big|_V
330	      \;=\;
331	    \mathcal{N}\!\left(\,(t_{ij}\circ f_{ji})(f_{ij}^{-1}V) \;=\; \iota_j^{-1}(\iota_i V)\,\right).
332	  \]
333	\end{lemma}
334	\begin{proof}
335	  \uses{lem:glueOverlapBaseChangeIso}
336	  \leanok
337	  Each of the four functorial factors of \(\beta_{ij}\) (\cref{lem:glueOverlapBaseChangeIso})
338	  acts on sections over \(V\) either as the identity or as a single presheaf
339	  restriction along the opens identity; composing them leaves exactly the named
340	  restriction map. The verification is pointwise on sections.
341	\end{proof}
342	
343	\begin{lemma}\leanok
344	[Sections of the overlap base change, forward side]
345	  \label{lem:gr_overlapBaseChange_hom_app}
346	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.glueOverlapBaseChangeIso_hom_app_app}
347	  \uses{lem:glueOverlapBaseChangeIso}
348	  Symmetrically, the forward overlap base change \(\beta_{ij}\), evaluated on sections
349	  over an open \(V \subseteq U_i\), is the restriction map of \(\mathcal{N}\) along the
350	  reverse opens identity \(\iota_j^{-1}(\iota_i(V)) = (t_{ij}\circ f_{ji})(f_{ij}^{-1}(V))\).
351	\end{lemma}
352	\begin{proof}
353	  \uses{lem:glueOverlapBaseChangeIso}
354	  \leanok
355	  Identical to \cref{lem:gr_overlapBaseChange_inv_app} with the roles of source and
356	  target opens exchanged: pointwise on sections, the composite of the four factors is
357	  the single presheaf restriction along the (reversed) opens identity.
358	\end{proof}
359	
360	\subsection{Bridges between the geometric and the site-level adjunction}
361	
362	The chart pullback \(\iota^{*}\) is built by sheafification and carries the
363	\emph{geometric} adjunction \(\iota^{*} \dashv \iota_{*}\); along an open immersion the
364	\emph{site-level} restriction functor \(\iota^{\mathrm{res}}\) carries a second
365	adjunction with concrete unit and counit. They share the right adjoint \(\iota_{*}\),
366	and \cref{lem:modules_restrictFunctorIsoPullback_mathlib} is the uniqueness comparison
367	of left adjoints between them. The next blocks transport units, counits and
368	congruence casts across that comparison, so that the descent obligations can be
369	discharged by section-level computations.
370	
371	\begin{lemma}\leanok
372	[Unit comparison across the adjunctions]
373	  \label{lem:gr_restrictAdjunction_unit_iso}
374	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.restrictAdjunction_unit_app_iso}
375	  \uses{lem:modules_restrictFunctorIsoPullback_mathlib}
376	  For an open immersion \(f\) and a sheaf \(\mathcal{N}\) on the target, the geometric
377	  unit is the site-level unit followed by the pushforward of the left-adjoint
378	  comparison isomorphism:
379	  \[
380	    \eta^{\mathrm{res}}_{\mathcal{N}}\ \circ\
381	      f_{*}\bigl((\text{comparison})_{\mathcal{N}}\bigr)
382	    \;=\; \eta^{\mathrm{geo}}_{\mathcal{N}}.
383	  \]
384	\end{lemma}
385	\begin{proof}
386	  \uses{lem:modules_restrictFunctorIsoPullback_mathlib}
387	  \leanok
388	  This is the general identity relating the unit of an adjunction to the unit of any
389	  other adjunction with the same right adjoint, transported by the uniqueness
390	  isomorphism of left adjoints (\cref{lem:modules_restrictFunctorIsoPullback_mathlib}).
391	\end{proof}
392	
393	\begin{lemma}\leanok
394	[Counit comparison across the adjunctions]
395	  \label{lem:gr_restrictIsoPullback_counit}
396	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.restrictFunctorIsoPullback_hom_app_counit}
397	  \uses{lem:modules_restrictFunctorIsoPullback_mathlib}
398	  Dually, the left-adjoint comparison isomorphism on \(f_{*}\mathcal{N}\) followed by
399	  the geometric counit equals the site-level counit:
400	  \[
401	    (\text{comparison})_{f_{*}\mathcal{N}}\ \circ\ \varepsilon^{\mathrm{geo}}_{\mathcal{N}}
402	      \;=\; \varepsilon^{\mathrm{res}}_{\mathcal{N}}.
403	  \]
404	\end{lemma}
405	\begin{proof}
406	  \uses{lem:modules_restrictFunctorIsoPullback_mathlib}
407	  \leanok
408	  The dual of \cref{lem:gr_restrictAdjunction_unit_iso}: the counit of one adjunction
409	  is conjugated to the counit of the other by the uniqueness comparison of left
410	  adjoints.
411	\end{proof}
412	
413	\begin{lemma}\leanok
414	[Congruence cast at the pullback level]
415	  \label{lem:gr_pullbackCongr_hom_eqToHom}
416	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.pullbackCongr_hom_app_eqToHom}
417	  For equal morphisms \(\varphi = \psi : X \to Y\) and a sheaf \(\mathcal{N}\) on \(Y\),
418	  the congruence isomorphism \(\varphi^{*}\mathcal{N} \cong \psi^{*}\mathcal{N}\)
419	  induced by \(\varphi = \psi\) is the canonical transport (the \(\mathrm{eqToHom}\) of
420	  the object-level equality \(\varphi^{*}\mathcal{N} = \psi^{*}\mathcal{N}\)).
421	\end{lemma}
422	\begin{proof}
423	
424	\leanok
425	  Substituting \(\varphi = \psi\) reduces the congruence cast to the identity, which is
426	  the canonical transport of a reflexivity.
427	\end{proof}
428	
429	\begin{lemma}\leanok
430	[Trivial site-level congruence cast]
431	  \label{lem:gr_restrictFunctorCongr_rfl}
432	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.restrictFunctorCongr_rfl_hom_app}
433	  For an open immersion \(\varphi\) the site-level congruence cast associated to the
434	  reflexivity \(\varphi = \varphi\) is the identity natural transformation.
435	\end{lemma}
436	\begin{proof}
437	
438	\leanok
439	  Pointwise on sections the cast is the presheaf restriction along a self-equality of
440	  opens, which is the identity by functoriality.
441	\end{proof}
442	
443	\begin{lemma}\leanok
444	[Comparison intertwines the two congruence casts]
445	  \label{lem:gr_restrictIsoPullback_congr}
446	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.restrictFunctorIsoPullback_congr}
447	  \uses{lem:gr_pullbackCongr_hom_eqToHom, lem:gr_restrictFunctorCongr_rfl,
448	    lem:modules_restrictFunctorIsoPullback_mathlib}
449	  For equal open immersions \(\varphi = \psi : X \to Y\) the left-adjoint comparison
450	  isomorphism intertwines the site-level and the pullback-level congruence casts:
451	  \[
452	    (\text{comparison}_\varphi)_{\mathcal{N}}\ \circ\
453	      (\varphi^{*}\!=\psi^{*})_{\mathcal{N}}
454	    \;=\;
455	    (\varphi^{\mathrm{res}}\!=\psi^{\mathrm{res}})_{\mathcal{N}}\ \circ\
456	      (\text{comparison}_\psi)_{\mathcal{N}}.
457	  \]
458	\end{lemma}
459	\begin{proof}
460	  \uses{lem:gr_pullbackCongr_hom_eqToHom, lem:gr_restrictFunctorCongr_rfl}
461	  \leanok
462	  Substituting \(\varphi = \psi\) collapses both congruence casts to identities
463	  (\cref{lem:gr_pullbackCongr_hom_eqToHom}, \cref{lem:gr_restrictFunctorCongr_rfl}),
464	  and the two comparison isomorphisms then coincide.
465	\end{proof}
466	
467	\subsection{The candidate inverse}
468	
469	\begin{definition}\leanok
470	[Overlap base change in pullback form]
471	  \label{def:gr_glueOverlapFactorIso}
472	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.glueOverlapFactorIso}
473	  \uses{lem:glueOverlapBaseChangeIso, lem:modules_restrictFunctorIsoPullback_mathlib}
474	  Conjugating the overlap base change \(\beta_{ij}\)
475	  (\cref{lem:glueOverlapBaseChangeIso}) on both sides by the comparison
476	  \cref{lem:modules_restrictFunctorIsoPullback_mathlib} between the site-level
477	  restriction and the geometric pullback yields the isomorphism, in geometric form,
478	  \[
479	    \gamma_{ij} \;:\;
480	      \iota_i^{*}\bigl((\iota_j)_{*}\,\mathcal{M}_j\bigr)
481	        \;\xrightarrow{\ \sim\ }\;
482	      (f_{ij})_{*}\bigl((t_{ij}\circ f_{ji})^{*}\,\mathcal{M}_j\bigr),
483	  \]
484	  evaluated at the input sheaf \(\mathcal{M}_j\).
485	\end{definition}
486	
487	\begin{definition}\leanok
488	[The \(j\)-th component of the candidate inverse]
489	  \label{def:gr_glueChartComponent}
490	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.glueChartComponent}
491	  \uses{def:gr_glueOverlapFactorIso}
492	  Given the transition isomorphisms \(g_{ij} : f_{ij}^{*}\mathcal{M}_i \xrightarrow{\sim}
493	  (t_{ij}\circ f_{ji})^{*}\mathcal{M}_j\), the \(j\)-th component of the candidate
494	  inverse \(\mathrm{s}_i\) is the composite
495	  \[
496	    \mathcal{M}_i
497	      \xrightarrow{\ \eta_{f_{ij}}\ }
498	    (f_{ij})_{*}\,f_{ij}^{*}\mathcal{M}_i
499	      \xrightarrow{\ (f_{ij})_{*}g_{ij}\ }
500	    (f_{ij})_{*}\,(t_{ij}\circ f_{ji})^{*}\mathcal{M}_j
501	      \xrightarrow{\ \gamma_{ij}^{-1}\ }
502	    \iota_i^{*}\bigl((\iota_j)_{*}\mathcal{M}_j\bigr),
503	  \]
504	  i.e. the geometric unit along \(f_{ij}\), the pushforward of the transition
505	  \(g_{ij}\), and the inverse of \(\gamma_{ij}\) (\cref{def:gr_glueOverlapFactorIso}).
506	\end{definition}
507	
508	\begin{definition}\leanok
509	[The candidate-inverse family]
510	  \label{def:gr_glueChartFamily}
511	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.glueChartFamily}
512	  \uses{def:gr_glueChartComponent, def:gr_glue_equalizer}
513	  Assembling the components \cref{def:gr_glueChartComponent} through the
514	  product-preservation comparison of \(\iota_i^{*}\) on the pushforward product
515	  \(P = \prod_j (\iota_j)_{*}\mathcal{M}_j\) (\cref{def:gr_glue_equalizer}) gives a
516	  single morphism
517	  \[
518	    \mathcal{M}_i \;\longrightarrow\; \iota_i^{*}P,
519	  \]
520	  the candidate inverse before it is corrected to land in the equalizer
521	  \(\iota_i^{*}\mathcal{M}\).
522	\end{definition}
523	
524	\subsection{The triple-overlap toolkit}
525	
526	The first descent obligation (\cref{lem:gr_glueChartFamily_equalizes}) reduces, factor
527	by factor of the restricted overlap product, to a single equation over a \emph{triple
528	overlap}. This subsection records that machinery. Fix three chart indices \(i, p, q\)
529	and form the triple overlap
530	\[
531	  V_{ipq} \;=\; V_{ip} \times_{U_i} V_{iq},
532	\]
533	with first projection \(\mathrm{fst} : V_{ipq} \to V_{ip}\). Two morphisms out of
534	\(V_{ipq}\) organise everything: the \emph{chart leg}
535	\(q_{pq} = \mathrm{fst}\circ f_{ip} : V_{ipq} \to U_i\) (down to \(U_i\)), and the
536	\emph{transport leg} \(\tau = t'_{ipq}\circ \mathrm{fst}_{pq} : V_{ipq} \to V_{pq}\)
537	(across to the pair overlap, through the cocycle transport \(t'_{ipq}\)). The square
538	\[
539	  \begin{array}{ccc}
540	    V_{ipq} & \xrightarrow{\ \ \tau\ \ } & V_{pq} \\
541	    \ \downarrow{\scriptstyle q_{pq}} & & \downarrow{\scriptstyle f_{pq}\circ \iota_p} \\
542	    U_i & \xrightarrow{\ \ \iota_i\ \ } & X
543	  \end{array}
544	\]
545	is the triple analogue of the chart--overlap square of \cref{lem:glueOverlapBaseChangeIso}.
546	
547	\begin{lemma}
548	[Triple-overlap bridges of the glue datum]
549	  \label{lem:gr_glueData_bridges}
550	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.glueData_bridge_src,
551	    AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.glueData_bridge_mid,
552	    AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.glueData_bridge_tgt}
553	  \uses{def:scheme_modules_glue}
554	  On the triple overlap \(V_{ijk} = V_{ij}\times_{U_i} V_{ik}\) the following three
555	  identities of morphisms hold, lining up the endpoints of the three base-change
556	  transports of the cocycle equation (C2):
557	  \begin{enumerate}
558	    \item \emph{(source)} the two projections of \(V_{ijk}\) to \(V_{ij}\) and \(V_{ik}\),
559	      followed by the overlap immersions \(f_{ij}\) and \(f_{ik}\) into \(U_i\),
560	      agree;
561	    \item \emph{(middle)} the \(ij\)-transport's target leg
562	      \((t_{ij}\circ f_{ji})\) and the \(jk\)-transport's source leg
563	      (through the cocycle transport \(t'_{ijk}\) and \(f_{jk}\)) agree as morphisms
564	      to \(U_j\);
565	    \item \emph{(target)} the composite \(jk\)-after-\(ij\) transport's target leg
566	      \((t_{jk}\circ f_{kj})\) and the \(ik\)-transport's target leg
567	      \((t_{ik}\circ f_{ki})\) agree as morphisms to \(U_k\).
568	  \end{enumerate}
569	\end{lemma}
570	\begin{proof}
571	  \uses{def:scheme_modules_glue}
572	  The source identity is the universal property (pullback condition) of \(V_{ijk}\).
573	  The middle identity follows from the transition factorisation
574	  \(t'_{ijk}\) of \cref{def:scheme_modules_glue} together with the pullback condition.
575	  The target identity -- the heart of the cocycle -- combines the same transition
576	  factorisation, the pullback condition, the involutivity \(t_{ik}\circ t_{ki}=\mathrm{id}\)
577	  and the cocycle condition of \cref{def:scheme_modules_glue}.
578	\end{proof}
579	
580	\begin{lemma}
581	
582	\leanok
583	[Commutativity of the triple-overlap square]
584	  \label{lem:gr_glueData_triple_square}
585	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.glueData_triple_square}
586	  \uses{lem:gr_glueData_bridges, def:scheme_modules_glue}
587	  The triple-overlap square above commutes: the transport leg \(\tau\) followed by
588	  \(f_{pq}\circ \iota_p\) equals the chart leg \(q_{pq}\) followed by \(\iota_i\), as
589	  morphisms \(V_{ipq}\to X\).
590	\end{lemma}
591	\begin{proof}
592	  \uses{lem:gr_glueData_bridges, def:scheme_modules_glue}
593	  \leanok
594	  Substitute the middle bridge \cref{lem:gr_glueData_bridges} to rewrite the transport
595	  leg, then apply the glue condition \((t_{ip}\circ f_{pi})\circ\iota_p =
596	  f_{ip}\circ\iota_i\) of \cref{def:scheme_modules_glue}.
597	\end{proof}
598	
599	\begin{lemma}
600	
601	\leanok
602	[Triple-overlap opens identity]
603	  \label{lem:gr_glueData_triple_preimage}
604	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.glueData_preimage_image_eq₃}
605	  \uses{lem:gr_glueData_bridges, lem:gr_glueData_triple_square, def:scheme_modules_glue}
606	  For an open \(V \subseteq U_i\), the preimage under \(f_{pq}\circ\iota_p\) of the image
607	  \(\iota_i(V)\) in the glued scheme equals the image, under the transport leg \(\tau\),
608	  of the preimage \(q_{pq}^{-1}(V)\):
609	  \[
610	    (f_{pq}\circ\iota_p)^{-1}\bigl(\iota_i(V)\bigr)
611	      \;=\; \tau\bigl(q_{pq}^{-1}(V)\bigr).
612	  \]
613	  This is the underlying-opens cartesianness of the triple-overlap square.
614	\end{lemma}
615	\begin{proof}
616	  \uses{lem:gr_glueData_bridges, lem:gr_glueData_triple_square, def:scheme_modules_glue}
617	  \leanok
618	  A point of \(V_{pq}\) lands in \(\iota_i(V)\) precisely when it is glued, through the
619	  overlap \(V_{ip}\), to a point of \(U_i\) lying in \(V\); the gluing-incidence
620	  criterion at the pairs \((i,p)\) and \((i,q)\) certifies that such a point lifts to
621	  \(V_{ipq}\), and injectivity of the open immersion \(f_{pq}\) pins down the transport,
622	  using the middle bridge \cref{lem:gr_glueData_bridges}. The reverse inclusion is the
623	  commutativity \cref{lem:gr_glueData_triple_square}.
624	\end{proof}
625	
626	\begin{lemma}
627	
628	\leanok
629	[The two composite opens functors of the triple square agree]
630	  \label{lem:gr_glueData_triple_opensFunctor_eq}
631	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.glueData_triple_opensFunctor_eq}
632	  \uses{lem:gr_glueData_triple_preimage}
633	  The composite opens functor ``into the glued scheme along \(\iota_i\), then back to
634	  \(V_{pq}\) along \(f_{pq}\circ\iota_p\)'' equals the composite ``down to \(V_{ipq}\)
635	  along \(q_{pq}\), then across to \(V_{pq}\) along \(\tau\)''.
636	\end{lemma}
637	\begin{proof}
638	  \uses{lem:gr_glueData_triple_preimage}
639	  \leanok
640	  The two functors agree on objects by the opens identity
641	  \cref{lem:gr_glueData_triple_preimage}, and on morphisms because the connecting arrows
642	  of the opens poset are proof-irrelevant.
643	\end{proof}

645	\begin{lemma}
646	
647	\leanok
648	[Structure-sheaf compatibility of the triple square]

652	  For an open \(V \subseteq U_i\) the two composite section maps
653	  \(\Gamma(U_i, V) \to \Gamma\bigl(V_{pq}, \tau(q_{pq}^{-1}(V))\bigr)\) of the triple

662	  \cref{lem:gr_overlap_appIso_compat}.
663	\end{proof}

665	\begin{lemma}
666	
667	\leanok
668	[Triple-overlap base change (\(\beta_{ipq}\))]
669	  \label{lem:gr_glueTripleBaseChangeIso}
670	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.glueTripleBaseChangeIso}

675	  \[

677	      (f_{pq}\circ\iota_p)_{*}\circ \iota_i^{*}
678	        \;\xrightarrow{\ \sim\ }\;

680	  \]
681	  identifying the chart restriction of a pushforward from the pair overlap \(V_{pq}\)

692	  \cref{lem:gr_glueData_triple_opensFunctor_eq}; the comparison is the pushforward-com\-position
693	  isomorphisms together with the transport of one opens functor to the other, with the

695	\end{proof}

883	[Pair component of the equalizing condition (C2 over the triple overlap)]

899	\begin{proof}

838	                (D.t i j ≫ D.f j i)).unit.app (M j)) ≫

891	/-! ### Restriction of the glued sheaf to a chart (`def:gr_modules_glueRestrictionIso`)
892	
893	The glued sheaf is the descent equalizer `eq(a, b) ⊆ ∏ᵢ (ιᵢ)_* Mᵢ`. This section
894	re-exposes the two legs of that equalizer as standalone declarations
895	(`glueLegA`/`glueLegB`), records that `glue` *is* their equalizer (`glueIsoEqualizer`,
896	definitional), and produces the canonical projection `glueProj i` of the glued sheaf
897	onto the `i`-th pushforward factor together with its compatibility with `glueLift`.
898	The adjoint transpose of `glueProj i` along the chart immersion `D.ι i` is the
899	*restriction morphism* `glueRestrictionHom i : ιᵢ^* (glue …) ⟶ M i`; effective descent
900	(consuming the cocycle hypotheses C1/C2) makes it an isomorphism — the restriction
901	isomorphism `glueRestrictionIso` of `def:gr_modules_glueRestrictionIso`. The
902	limit-preservation engine is `restrictFunctor`: pullback along an open immersion is
903	naturally isomorphic to a site-level pushforward, which is a right adjoint, hence
904	preserves the descent equalizer and the pushforward product. -/
905	
906	/-- `restrictFunctor f` along an open immersion is a right adjoint: it is a site-level
907	pushforward of sheaves of modules, whose left adjoint (the site-level pullback) exists
908	by the presheaf-pullback + sheafification construction. Project-local instance. -/
909	instance restrictFunctor_isRightAdjoint {X Y : Scheme.{u}} (f : X ⟶ Y) [IsOpenImmersion f] :
910	    (restrictFunctor f).IsRightAdjoint := by
911	  delta restrictFunctor
912	  -- bare `infer_instance` fails on the outer search; the explicit presheaf-pullback +
913	  -- sheafification construction elaborates (its three instance hypotheses all resolve)
914	  exact (SheafOfModules.PullbackConstruction.adjunction _).isRightAdjoint
915	
916	/-- `restrictFunctor f` along an open immersion preserves limits (it is a right
917	adjoint). Project-local. -/
918	noncomputable instance restrictFunctor_preservesLimits.{w, w'} {X Y : Scheme.{u}}
919	    (f : X ⟶ Y) [IsOpenImmersion f] :
920	    PreservesLimitsOfSize.{w, w'} (restrictFunctor f) :=
921	  (Adjunction.ofIsRightAdjoint (restrictFunctor f)).rightAdjoint_preservesLimits
922	
923	/-- **Pullback of sheaves of modules along an open immersion preserves limits**: it is
924	naturally isomorphic to `restrictFunctor f`, a site-level pushforward and right
925	adjoint. This is the engine that lets the chart restriction commute with the descent
926	equalizer. Project-local. -/
927	instance pullback_preservesLimits_of_isOpenImmersion.{w, w'} {X Y : Scheme.{u}}
928	    (f : X ⟶ Y) [IsOpenImmersion f] :
929	    PreservesLimitsOfSize.{w, w'} (Scheme.Modules.pullback f) :=
930	  preservesLimits_of_natIso (restrictFunctorIsoPullback f)
931	
932	section GlueRestriction
933	
934	-- NOTE: `glue`/`glueLift` elaborated universe-monomorphic at `Scheme.GlueData.{0}`
935	-- (their universe was pinned during elaboration); the restriction layer follows suit.
936	variable (D : Scheme.GlueData.{0}) (M : ∀ i, (D.U i).Modules)
937	
938	/-- The product of pushforwards `∏ᵢ (ιᵢ)_* Mᵢ` into which the glued sheaf embeds.
939	Project-local helper re-exposing the `P` of `glue`. -/
940	noncomputable def glueProd : D.glued.Modules :=
941	  ∏ᶜ fun i => (Scheme.Modules.pushforward (D.ι i)).obj (M i)
942	
943	/-- The overlap product `∏_{(i,j)} (f_ij ≫ ιᵢ)_* (f_ij^* Mᵢ)` receiving the two descent
944	legs. Project-local helper re-exposing the `Qfun`-product of `glue`. -/
945	noncomputable def glueOverlapProd : D.glued.Modules :=
946	  ∏ᶜ fun p : D.J × D.J =>
947	    (Scheme.Modules.pushforward (D.f p.1 p.2 ≫ D.ι p.1)).obj
948	      ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (D.f p.1 p.2)).obj (M p.1))
949	
950	/-- First descent leg (`a` of `glue`): on the `(i,j)`-component, restrict the `i`-th
951	chart section to the overlap `V (i,j)` via the unit of the pullback–pushforward
952	adjunction along `f_ij` and the pushforward-composition comparison. Project-local. -/
953	noncomputable def glueLegA : glueProd D M ⟶ glueOverlapProd D M :=
954	  Pi.lift fun p => Pi.π _ p.1 ≫
955	    ((Scheme.Modules.pushforward (D.ι p.1)).map
956	        ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction (D.f p.1 p.2)).unit.app (M p.1)) ≫
957	      (Scheme.Modules.pushforwardComp (D.f p.1 p.2) (D.ι p.1)).hom.app
958	        ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (D.f p.1 p.2)).obj (M p.1)))
959	
960	/-- Second descent leg (`b` of `glue`): on the `(i,j)`-component, restrict the `j`-th
961	chart section, transport it across the transition isomorphism `g_ij`, and reindex the
962	immersion via the glue condition. Project-local. -/
963	noncomputable def glueLegB
964	    (g : ∀ i j, (Scheme.Modules.pullback (D.f i j)).obj (M i) ≅
965	        (Scheme.Modules.pullback (D.t i j ≫ D.f j i)).obj (M j)) :
966	    glueProd D M ⟶ glueOverlapProd D M :=
967	  Pi.lift fun p => Pi.π _ p.2 ≫
968	    ((Scheme.Modules.pushforward (D.ι p.2)).map
969	        ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction
970	          (D.t p.1 p.2 ≫ D.f p.2 p.1)).unit.app (M p.2)) ≫
971	      (Scheme.Modules.pushforwardComp (D.t p.1 p.2 ≫ D.f p.2 p.1) (D.ι p.2)).hom.app
972	        ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (D.t p.1 p.2 ≫ D.f p.2 p.1)).obj (M p.2)) ≫
973	      (Scheme.Modules.pushforward
974	        ((D.t p.1 p.2 ≫ D.f p.2 p.1) ≫ D.ι p.2)).map (g p.1 p.2).inv ≫
975	      (Scheme.Modules.pushforwardCongr
976	        (show (D.t p.1 p.2 ≫ D.f p.2 p.1) ≫ D.ι p.2 = D.f p.1 p.2 ≫ D.ι p.1 by
977	          rw [Category.assoc]; exact D.glue_condition p.1 p.2)).hom.app
978	        ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (D.f p.1 p.2)).obj (M p.1)))
979	
980	variable (g : ∀ i j, (Scheme.Modules.pullback (D.f i j)).obj (M i) ≅
981	      (Scheme.Modules.pullback (D.t i j ≫ D.f j i)).obj (M j))
982	  (hC1 : ∀ i, g i i = eqToIso (congrArg (fun φ => (Scheme.Modules.pullback φ).obj (M i))
983	      (show D.f i i = D.t i i ≫ D.f i i by rw [D.t_id i, Category.id_comp])))
984	  (hC2 : ∀ i j k,
985	      pullbackBaseChangeTransport (pullback.fst (D.f i j) (D.f i k))
986	          (D.f i j) (D.t i j ≫ D.f j i) (g i j) ≪≫
987	        (Scheme.Modules.pullbackCongr (glueData_bridge_mid D i j k)).app (M j) ≪≫
988	        pullbackBaseChangeTransport (D.t' i j k ≫ pullback.fst (D.f j k) (D.f j i))
989	          (D.f j k) (D.t j k ≫ D.f k j) (g j k) ≪≫
990	        (Scheme.Modules.pullbackCongr (glueData_bridge_tgt D i j k)).app (M k)
991	      = (Scheme.Modules.pullbackCongr (glueData_bridge_src D i j k)).app (M i) ≪≫
992	        pullbackBaseChangeTransport (pullback.snd (D.f i j) (D.f i k))
993	          (D.f i k) (D.t i k ≫ D.f k i) (g i k))
994	
995	/-- The glued sheaf *is* the equalizer of the two (re-exposed) descent legs. The
996	isomorphism is definitional (`Iso.refl`); it exists to let the equalizer API fire
997	syntactically on `glue`. Project-local. -/
998	noncomputable def glueIsoEqualizer :
999	    glue D M g hC1 hC2 ≅ equalizer (glueLegA D M) (glueLegB D M g) :=
1000	  Iso.refl _
1001	
1002	/-- Projection of the glued sheaf onto the `i`-th pushforward factor `(ιᵢ)_* Mᵢ`:
1003	the equalizer inclusion followed by the product projection. Project-local. -/
1004	noncomputable def glueProj (i : D.J) :
1005	    glue D M g hC1 hC2 ⟶ (Scheme.Modules.pushforward (D.ι i)).obj (M i) :=
1006	  (glueIsoEqualizer D M g hC1 hC2).hom ≫ equalizer.ι (glueLegA D M) (glueLegB D M g) ≫
1007	    Pi.π _ i

1009	/-- `glueLift` followed by the `i`-th projection recovers the `i`-th component of the
1010	lifted family. Project-local. -/
1011	@[reassoc]
1012	lemma glueLift_glueProj {W : D.glued.Modules}
1013	    (k : ∀ i, W ⟶ (Scheme.Modules.pushforward (D.ι i)).obj (M i))
1014	    (hk : ∀ p : D.J × D.J,
1015	      k p.1 ≫
1016	          ((Scheme.Modules.pushforward (D.ι p.1)).map
1017	            ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction (D.f p.1 p.2)).unit.app (M p.1)) ≫
1018	          (Scheme.Modules.pushforwardComp (D.f p.1 p.2) (D.ι p.1)).hom.app
1019	            ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (D.f p.1 p.2)).obj (M p.1)))
1020	        = k p.2 ≫
1021	          ((Scheme.Modules.pushforward (D.ι p.2)).map
1022	            ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction
1023	              (D.t p.1 p.2 ≫ D.f p.2 p.1)).unit.app (M p.2)) ≫
1024	          (Scheme.Modules.pushforwardComp (D.t p.1 p.2 ≫ D.f p.2 p.1) (D.ι p.2)).hom.app
1025	            ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (D.t p.1 p.2 ≫ D.f p.2 p.1)).obj (M p.2)) ≫
1026	          (Scheme.Modules.pushforward
1027	            ((D.t p.1 p.2 ≫ D.f p.2 p.1) ≫ D.ι p.2)).map (g p.1 p.2).inv ≫
1028	          (Scheme.Modules.pushforwardCongr
1029	            (show (D.t p.1 p.2 ≫ D.f p.2 p.1) ≫ D.ι p.2 = D.f p.1 p.2 ≫ D.ι p.1 by
1030	              rw [Category.assoc]; exact D.glue_condition p.1 p.2)).hom.app
1031	            ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (D.f p.1 p.2)).obj (M p.1)))) (i : D.J) :
1032	    glueLift D M g hC1 hC2 k hk ≫ glueProj D M g hC1 hC2 i = k i := by
1033	  dsimp only [glueLift, glueProj, glueIsoEqualizer, Iso.refl_hom]
1034	  -- term-mode: the mixed-provenance comp nodes block positional `rw [Category.id_comp]`
1035	  exact (congrArg (equalizer.lift (Pi.lift k) _ ≫ ·) (Category.id_comp _)).trans
1036	    ((Category.assoc _ _ _).symm.trans
1037	      ((congrArg (· ≫ Pi.π _ i) (equalizer.lift_ι _ _)).trans (Limits.Pi.lift_π _ _)))

1039	/-- **The restriction morphism of the glued sheaf** to the `i`-th chart: the adjoint
1040	transpose, along the chart immersion `ιᵢ`, of the `i`-th projection `glueProj i`.
1041	Effective descent (`isIso_glueRestrictionHom`) makes it an isomorphism. Project-local. -/
1042	noncomputable def glueRestrictionHom (i : D.J) :
1043	    (Scheme.Modules.pullback (D.ι i)).obj (glue D M g hC1 hC2) ⟶ M i :=
1044	  ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction (D.ι i)).homEquiv _ _).symm
1045	    (glueProj D M g hC1 hC2 i)

1047	/-- **The chart restriction of the glued sheaf is the equalizer of the restricted
1048	descent legs**: the chart pullback preserves the descent equalizer
1049	(`pullback_preservesLimits_of_isOpenImmersion`). First reduction step of
1050	`isIso_glueRestrictionHom`. Project-local. -/
1051	noncomputable def glueRestrictEqualizerIso (i : D.J) :
1052	    (Scheme.Modules.pullback (D.ι i)).obj (glue D M g hC1 hC2)
1053	      ≅ equalizer ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (D.ι i)).map (glueLegA D M))
1054	          ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (D.ι i)).map (glueLegB D M g)) :=
1055	  (Scheme.Modules.pullback (D.ι i)).mapIso (glueIsoEqualizer D M g hC1 hC2) ≪≫
1056	    PreservesEqualizer.iso (Scheme.Modules.pullback (D.ι i)) _ _

1058	/-- **The chart restriction of the pushforward product is the product of the
1059	restrictions**: the chart pullback preserves the product
1060	(`pullback_preservesLimits_of_isOpenImmersion`). Second reduction step of
1061	`isIso_glueRestrictionHom`: the factors `ι_i^* ((ι_j)_* M_j)` are then identified with
1062	`(f_ij)_* ((t_ij ≫ f_ji)^* M_j)` by the overlap base change of the cartesian chart
1063	square (`glueData_preimage_image_eq`). Project-local. -/
1064	noncomputable def glueRestrictProdIso (i : D.J) :
1065	    (Scheme.Modules.pullback (D.ι i)).obj (glueProd D M)
1066	      ≅ ∏ᶜ fun j => (Scheme.Modules.pullback (D.ι i)).obj
1067	          ((Scheme.Modules.pushforward (D.ι j)).obj (M j)) :=
1068	  PreservesProduct.iso (Scheme.Modules.pullback (D.ι i)) _

1075	lemma pullback_map_glueLift_glueRestrictionHom {W : D.glued.Modules}
1076	    (k : ∀ i, W ⟶ (Scheme.Modules.pushforward (D.ι i)).obj (M i))
1077	    (hk : ∀ p : D.J × D.J,
1078	      k p.1 ≫
1079	          ((Scheme.Modules.pushforward (D.ι p.1)).map
1080	            ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction (D.f p.1 p.2)).unit.app (M p.1)) ≫

1082	            ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (D.f p.1 p.2)).obj (M p.1)))
1083	        = k p.2 ≫
1084	          ((Scheme.Modules.pushforward (D.ι p.2)).map
1085	            ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction

1431	
1432	`Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction f` (the geometric adjunction, with the

1456	        (Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction f).counit.app N

1852	/-! ### Pseudofunctor cast coherence for the conjugated cocycle
1853	
1854	The remaining content of the descent obligation (`glueChartComponent_leg_compat`) is a
1855	comparison of two regroupings of iterated pullbacks: the route through the glued scheme
1856	(the triple-square casts) against the route through the chart overlaps (the pair-square
1857	casts whiskered by the triple-overlap projections). This comparison is *pure cast
1858	coherence*: it involves only the pseudofunctor comparisons `pullbackComp` and the
1859	congruence casts `pullbackCongr` — no units, counits, or transition isomorphisms. The
1860	block below derives it (`pullback_cast_compat`) from the Mathlib associativity
1861	2-cocycle `Scheme.Modules.pseudofunctor_associativity` specialised to objects, plus a
1862	handful of generic `subst`-style `pullbackComp`/`pullbackCongr` compatibilities. -/
1863	
1864	section CastCoherence
1865	
1866	/-- Generic 4-factor rearrangement: solve an iso-chain equation for its last factor.
1867	Project-local helper for the solved forms of the associativity cocycle. -/
1868	private lemma comp4_solve_last {𝒞 : Type*} [Category 𝒞] {a b c d e : 𝒞}
1869	    (A : a ≅ b) (B : b ≅ c) (Cc : c ≅ d) {D : d ⟶ e} {E : a ⟶ e}
1870	    (hE : A.hom ≫ B.hom ≫ Cc.hom ≫ D = E) :
1871	    D = Cc.inv ≫ B.inv ≫ A.inv ≫ E := by
1872	  rw [← hE]; simp

1875	Project-local. -/

1877	    (A : a ≅ b) {B : b ⟶ c} {Cc : c ⟶ d} (D : d ≅ e) {E : a ⟶ e}
1878	    (hE : A.hom ≫ B ≫ Cc ≫ D.hom = E) :
1879	    B ≫ Cc = A.inv ≫ E ≫ D.inv := by
1880	  rw [← hE]; simp

1886	    (hE : A ≫ B ≫ Cc.hom ≫ D.hom = E) :
1887	    A ≫ B = E ≫ D.inv ≫ Cc.inv := by
1888	  rw [← hE]; simp

1906	    (Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp f (g ≫ h)).inv.app M ≫
1907	        (Scheme.Modules.pullback f).map ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp g h).inv.app M) ≫
1908	        (Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp f g).hom.app ((Scheme.Modules.pullback h).obj M) ≫
1909	        (Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp (f ≫ g) h).hom.app M
1910	      = (Scheme.Modules.pullbackCongr (Category.assoc f g h).symm).hom.app M := by
1911	  have e := congrArg (fun α => α.app M)
1912	    (Scheme.Modules.pseudofunctor_associativity (f := f) (g := g) (h := h))
1913	  simp only [NatTrans.comp_app, Functor.whiskerRight_app, Functor.whiskerLeft_app,
1914	    Functor.associator_hom_app, eqToHom_app] at e
1915	  rw [pullbackCongr_hom_app_eqToHom]
1916	  exact e

2018	/-- Generic 5-factor pipeline rearrangement: substitute a middle-pair regrouping, a
2019	front-pair exchange and a back-pair exchange in one move. Stated in an abstract
2020	category (where `rw` matching is reliable) and applied to the concrete pullback chain
2021	by unification — the lever for the `X.Modules` instance diamond. Project-local. -/
2022	private lemma comp5_rearrange {𝒞 : Type*} [Category 𝒞]
2023	    {x₀ x₁ x₂ x₃ x₄ x₅ y₁ y₂ z₁ w : 𝒞}
2024	    {d₃ : x₀ ⟶ x₁} {d₄ : x₁ ⟶ x₂} {d₅ : x₂ ⟶ x₃} {d₆ : x₃ ⟶ x₄} {d₇ : x₄ ⟶ x₅}
2025	    {B : x₁ ⟶ y₁} {cC : y₁ ⟶ y₂} {e : y₂ ⟶ x₃} {A : x₀ ⟶ z₁} {cD : z₁ ⟶ y₁}
2026	    {cE : y₂ ⟶ w} {f : w ⟶ x₄}
2027	    (h45 : d₄ ≫ d₅ = B ≫ cC ≫ e) (h3 : d₃ ≫ B = A ≫ cD) (h6 : e ≫ d₆ = cE ≫ f) :
2028	    d₃ ≫ d₄ ≫ d₅ ≫ d₆ ≫ d₇ = A ≫ cD ≫ cC ≫ cE ≫ f ≫ d₇ := by
2029	  rw [reassoc_of% h45, reassoc_of% h6, ← Category.assoc d₃ B, h3, Category.assoc]

2031	/-- **Cast compatibility of the triple-overlap and pair-overlap regroupings** (the
2032	coherence core of the conjugated cocycle): given a pair square `t ≫ κ = a ≫ ι`, a
2033	middle bridge `u ≫ t = τ ≫ b` and the induced triple square
2034	`τ ≫ (b ≫ κ) = (u ≫ a) ≫ ι`, the cast chain through the triple square equals the
2035	pair-square cast chain pulled back along `u`, re-bracketed through the pseudofunctor
2036	associativity. Pure cast coherence — no units, counits, or transition isomorphisms.
2037	Project-local. -/
2038	lemma pullback_cast_compat {P A' B' C' E' X' : Scheme.{u}}
2039	    (u : P ⟶ A') (a : A' ⟶ C') (ι : C' ⟶ X') (t : A' ⟶ E') (κ : E' ⟶ X')
2040	    (τ : P ⟶ B') (b : B' ⟶ E')
2041	    (hpair : t ≫ κ = a ≫ ι) (hmid : u ≫ t = τ ≫ b)
2042	    (htriple : τ ≫ (b ≫ κ) = (u ≫ a) ≫ ι) (W : X'.Modules) :
2043	    (Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp (u ≫ a) ι).hom.app W ≫
2044	        (Scheme.Modules.pullbackCongr htriple).inv.app W ≫
2045	        (Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp τ (b ≫ κ)).inv.app W ≫
2046	        (Scheme.Modules.pullback τ).map ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp b κ).inv.app W)
2047	      = (Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp u a).inv.app ((Scheme.Modules.pullback ι).obj W) ≫
2048	        (Scheme.Modules.pullback u).map ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp a ι).hom.app W) ≫
2049	        (Scheme.Modules.pullback u).map ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackCongr hpair).inv.app W) ≫
2050	        (Scheme.Modules.pullback u).map ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp t κ).inv.app W) ≫
2051	        (Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp u t).hom.app ((Scheme.Modules.pullback κ).obj W) ≫
2052	        (Scheme.Modules.pullbackCongr hmid).hom.app ((Scheme.Modules.pullback κ).obj W) ≫
2053	        (Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp τ b).inv.app ((Scheme.Modules.pullback κ).obj W) := by
2054	  -- regroup the composite first leg on the left-hand side (`erw`: plain `rw` cannot
2055	  -- match the comp-node pattern under the `X.Modules` instance diamond)
2056	  erw [pullbackComp_comp_fst_hom_app_assoc u a ι W]
2057	  -- regroup the τ-side trailing pair on the left-hand side
2058	  rw [pullbackComp_inv_comp_map_inv_app τ b κ W]
2059	  -- strip the common two-factor prefix
2060	  refine whisker_eq _ (whisker_eq _ ?_)
2061	  -- the residual three-cast stacks agree under any continuation (all `pullbackCongr`
2062	  -- casts are `eqToHom`s; the fresh `have` is elaborated uniformly, so `simp` fires)
2063	  have hcast : ∀ {Z : P.Modules}
2064	      (zc : (Scheme.Modules.pullback ((τ ≫ b) ≫ κ)).obj W ⟶ Z),
2065	      (Scheme.Modules.pullbackCongr (Category.assoc u a ι).symm).hom.app W ≫
2066	          (Scheme.Modules.pullbackCongr htriple).inv.app W ≫
2067	          (Scheme.Modules.pullbackCongr (Category.assoc τ b κ).symm).hom.app W ≫ zc
2068	        = (Scheme.Modules.pullbackCongr
2069	              (show u ≫ t ≫ κ = u ≫ a ≫ ι by rw [hpair])).inv.app W ≫
2070	          (Scheme.Modules.pullbackCongr (Category.assoc u t κ).symm).hom.app W ≫
2071	          (Scheme.Modules.pullbackCongr
2072	              (show (u ≫ t) ≫ κ = (τ ≫ b) ≫ κ by rw [hmid])).hom.app W ≫ zc := by
2073	    intro Z zc
2074	    simp only [pullbackCongr_hom_app_eqToHom, pullbackCongr_inv_app_eqToHom,
2075	      eqToHom_trans_assoc]
2076	  -- right-hand side: regroup the middle pair (t, κ), commute the pair congruence past
2077	  -- the regrouping (second argument) and the middle bridge congruence past the
2078	  -- regrouping (first argument) — assembled by the abstract-category rearrangement
2079	  -- (the corresponding `rw`s cannot match these comp nodes under the diamond)
2080	  exact (whisker_eq _ (hcast _)).trans
2081	    (comp5_rearrange (pullback_map_inv_comp_hom_app u t κ W)
2082	      (pullback_map_congr_inv_comp_hom_app u hpair W)
2083	      (pullbackComp_inv_comp_congr_hom_app hmid κ W)).symm

2086	in abstract categories and applied by unification (the `X.Modules` diamond lever).

2090	    {k₄ : x₄ ⟶ x₅} {z : x₀ ⟶ x₅} (h : a ≫ k₁ ≫ k₂ ≫ k₃ ≫ k₄ = z) :
2091	    F.map a ≫ F.map k₁ ≫ F.map k₂ ≫ F.map k₃ ≫ F.map k₄ = F.map z := by
2092	  rw [← F.map_comp, ← F.map_comp, ← F.map_comp, ← F.map_comp, h]

2101	    {x₀ x₁ y₁ y₂ y₃ y₄ y₅ a₁ a₂ a₃ a₄ a₅ a₆ b₁ m₁ m₂ m₃ : 𝒞}
2102	    {X : x₀ ⟶ x₁} {c₁ : x₁ ⟶ y₁} {c₂ : y₁ ⟶ y₂} {c₃ : y₂ ⟶ y₃} {w : y₃ ⟶ y₅}
2103	    {c₄ : y₃ ⟶ y₄} {Y : y₄ ⟶ y₅}
2104	    {F1 : x₁ ⟶ a₁} {F2 : a₁ ⟶ a₂} {F3 : a₂ ⟶ a₃} {F4 : a₃ ⟶ a₄} {F5 : a₄ ⟶ a₅}
2105	    {F6 : a₅ ⟶ a₆} {F7 : a₆ ⟶ y₄}
2106	    {F1' : x₀ ⟶ b₁} {X' : b₁ ⟶ a₁}
2107	    {Y₁ : a₆ ⟶ m₁} {F7' : m₁ ⟶ y₅} {Y₂ : a₅ ⟶ m₂} {F6' : m₂ ⟶ m₁}
2108	    {Y₃ : a₄ ⟶ m₃} {F5' : m₃ ⟶ m₂} {Z : b₁ ⟶ m₃}
2109	    (hw : w = c₄ ≫ Y)

2136	    {x₀ x₁ s₁ s₂ y₁ y₂ y₃ z₁ z₂ z₃ y₆ e₁ a₁ a₂ a₃ a₄ a₅ a₆ b₀ b₁ m₁ m₂ m₃ : 𝒞}
2137	    {X : x₀ ⟶ x₁} {c₁ : x₁ ⟶ y₁} {c₂ : y₁ ⟶ y₂} {c₃ : y₂ ⟶ y₃} {w : y₃ ⟶ y₆}
2138	    {d₁ : y₃ ⟶ z₁} {d₂ : z₁ ⟶ z₂} {d₃ : z₂ ⟶ z₃} {d₄ : z₃ ⟶ y₆}

2140	    {ctq : s₂ ⟶ e₁}
2141	    {G1 : s₁ ⟶ a₁} {G2 : a₁ ⟶ a₂} {G3 : a₂ ⟶ a₃} {G4 : a₃ ⟶ a₄} {G5 : a₄ ⟶ a₅}
2142	    {G6 : a₅ ⟶ a₆} {G7 : a₆ ⟶ z₂}

2146	    (hw : w = d₁ ≫ d₂ ≫ d₃ ≫ d₄)
2147	    (hc₁ : c₁ = Cs ≫ c₁' ≫ ce₁)
2148	    (hex : c₃ ≫ d₁ = ce₂ ≫ c₃')
2149	    (hstack : ∀ {Z'} (zc : e₁ ⟶ Z'), ce₁ ≫ c₂ ≫ ce₂ ≫ zc = ctq ≫ zc)

2197	/-! ### The candidate inverse of the chart restriction morphism
2198	
2199	The inverse `σ_i : M i ⟶ ι_i^* (glue …)` is assembled as an equalizer lift: the chart
2200	pullback preserves the descent equalizer (`glueRestrictEqualizerIso`) and the
2201	pushforward product (`glueRestrictProdIso`), so a map into `ι_i^* (glue …)` is a
2202	family of maps into the factors `ι_i^* ((ι_j)_* M_j)` equalizing the restricted legs.
2203	The `j`-th component (`glueChartComponent`) transports a section of `M i` to the
2204	overlap: the unit along `f_ij`, the transition isomorphism `g_ij`, then the inverse of
2205	the overlap base change `β_ij` (`glueOverlapBaseChangeIso`, in pullback form
2206	`glueOverlapFactorIso`). The three named obligations consuming the cocycle hypotheses
2207	are `glueChartFamily_equalizes` (C2 transported), `glueChartComponent_self_counit`
2208	(C1 + the counit triangle), and `glueRestrictionHom_glueChartComponent` (the
2209	pair-`(i,j)` equalizer condition transposed). -/
2210	
2211	section GlueRestrictionInverse
2212	
2213	variable (D : Scheme.GlueData.{0}) (M : ∀ i, (D.U i).Modules)
2214	
2215	/-- **Object-level overlap base change in pullback form**: the `β_ij` of
2216	`glueOverlapBaseChangeIso`, evaluated at `M j` and conjugated through
2217	`restrictFunctorIsoPullback` on both sides, identifying
2218	`ι_i^* ((ι_j)_* M_j) ≅ (f_ij)_* ((t_ij ≫ f_ji)^* M_j)` with the geometric pullback
2219	functors. Project-local. -/
2220	noncomputable def glueOverlapFactorIso (i j : D.J) :
2221	    (Scheme.Modules.pullback (D.ι i)).obj ((Scheme.Modules.pushforward (D.ι j)).obj (M j))
2222	      ≅ (Scheme.Modules.pushforward (D.f i j)).obj
2223	          ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (D.t i j ≫ D.f j i)).obj (M j)) :=
2224	  (restrictFunctorIsoPullback (D.ι i)).symm.app
2225	      ((Scheme.Modules.pushforward (D.ι j)).obj (M j)) ≪≫
2226	    (glueOverlapBaseChangeIso D i j).app (M j) ≪≫
2227	    (Scheme.Modules.pushforward (D.f i j)).mapIso

  Let \(\mathcal{M}\) be the glued sheaf of \cref{def:scheme_modules_glue}. For each

    \rho_i \;:\; \iota_i^{*}\,\mathcal{M} \;\xrightarrow{\ \sim\ }\; \mathcal{M}_i,

  restrictions \(\rho_i\) and \(\rho_j\), transported to the common overlap, differ
  exactly by \(g_{ij}\).
\end{definition}
